div1.entryFree: Censer, a fire-pan in which perfumes were burned to sweeten the atmosphere; such had, of course, their lids perforated, and sometimes adorned with figures: here's snip and nip and cut and slish and slash, like to a c. in a barber's shop, Shr. IV, 3, 91. you thin man in a c. H4B V, 4, 21.
